# openshift_assisted_service (Resource)

Deploys the Assisted Installer service on a bastion host via SSH using podman. Use the api_url output as assisted_service_url in the openshift provider or openshift_cluster resource.



## Example Usage

### Deploy on bastion and use the URL in subsequent resources

The `api_url` output is the address at which nodes will reach the Assisted Installer during discovery. Pass it as `assisted_service_url` on the `openshift_cluster` resource (or at provider level if you split workspaces). The `depends_on` on `openshift_cluster` ensures the service is running before the cluster record is created.

```hcl
resource "openshift_assisted_service" "bastion" {
  bastion_host     = "bastion.example.internal"
  bastion_user     = "core"
  bastion_ssh_key  = file("~/.ssh/bastion_id_rsa")

  # Nodes call back to this URL during discovery — must be reachable from
  # the provisioning network, not just from the Terraform host.
  service_base_url = "http://bastion.example.internal:8090"

  # Optional: restrict to a specific OCP version in airgapped environments.
  ocp_versions = jsonencode({
    "4.16" = {
      display_name      = "4.16"
      release_image     = "bastion.example.internal:8443/openshift-release-dev/ocp-release:4.16.0-x86_64"
      release_version   = "4.16.0"
      support_level     = "production"
    }
  })
}

resource "openshift_cluster" "prod" {
  depends_on = [openshift_assisted_service.bastion]

  cluster_name         = "prod"
  base_dns_domain      = "example.internal"
  openshift_version    = "4.16"
  pull_secret          = var.pull_secret
  ssh_public_key       = var.ssh_public_key

  api_vip              = "192.168.10.10"
  ingress_vip          = "192.168.10.11"
  machine_network_cidr = "192.168.10.0/24"

  # Override provider-level URL to point at the self-hosted service.
  assisted_service_url = openshift_assisted_service.bastion.api_url

  create_infra_env = true
  auto_install     = false
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `bastion_host` (String) Hostname or IP of the bastion host.
- `bastion_ssh_key` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded SSH private key for connecting to the bastion host.
- `service_base_url` (String) URL at which the Assisted Service will be reachable from the nodes, e.g. http://bastion.example.internal:8090.

### Optional

- `bastion_port` (Number) SSH port on the bastion host.
- `bastion_user` (String) SSH user on the bastion host. Defaults to 'core' (RHCOS/Fedora CoreOS).
- `http_proxy` (String) HTTP proxy for the Assisted Service to reach the internet (if needed).
- `https_proxy` (String) HTTPS proxy for the Assisted Service.
- `mirror_registry_ca` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded CA certificate for the mirror registry.
- `mirror_registry_url` (String) Mirror registry URL for airgapped deployments, e.g. bastion.example.internal:8443.
- `no_proxy` (String) Comma-separated list of hosts that bypass the proxy.
- `ocp_versions` (String) JSON map of supported OCP versions passed to the Assisted Service.

### Read-Only

- `api_url` (String) Base URL of the deployed Assisted Installer API. Use as assisted_service_url in the provider or openshift_cluster.
